name: BLUE DOLPHIN ENERGY CO
summary: Blue Dolphin Energy Company is a petroleum refining and marketing company operating the Nixon facility in Nixon, Texas, which includes a 15,000-bpd crude distillation tower. The company refines crude oil and condensate into finished and intermediate petroleum products including jet fuel, low-sulfur diesel, naphtha, heavy oil-based mud blendstock (HOBM), and atmospheric gas oil (AGO). Blue Dolphin also provides tolling and terminaling services and owns an inactive onshore terminal facility in Freeport, Texas.

products.name: Jet Fuel
products.description: High-quality kerosene product primarily used in aviation, produced at the Nixon refinery.

products.name: Low-Sulfur Diesel
products.description: Finished petroleum distillate product produced at the Nixon refinery crude distillation tower.

products.name: Naphtha
products.description: Light distillate fraction of crude oil used as feedstock or blended for gasoline or jet fuel.

products.name: Heavy Oil-Based Mud Blendstock (HOBM)
products.description: Heavy distillate intermediate petroleum product produced at the Nixon refinery.

products.name: Atmospheric Gas Oil (AGO)
products.description: Heaviest product from the crude distillation tower; sold as distillate fuel oil or blended with cracked stocks.

products.name: Tolling and Terminaling Services
products.description: Processing and terminal services provided under tolling agreements, including intercompany naphtha processing fees.

partnerships.description: Affiliate and controlling shareholder; LEH and Jonathan Carroll together owned 84.4% of Common Stock as of filing date.
Affiliate of LEH involved in terminal operations related to Blue Dolphin's business.
Banking relationship referenced in the filing as a financial institution.
Government agency referenced as a customer or counterparty for fuel supply.
